results.title: DBS‑backed family‑office platform hits $780 million in assets, to double by end‑2026 - Reuters
results.content: SINGAPORE, Sept 23 (Reuters) - DBS Private Bank, part of Singapore's biggest bank DBS Group (DBSM.SI), opens new tab, said on Tuesday its bank-backed multi-family office platform has reached S$1 billion ($780 million) in assets under management two years after launch and is on track to hit S$2 billion by end-2026. Yantoultra Ngui is the Southeast Asia Deals Correspondent of Reuters in Singapore, covering M&A and capital market activities in a region that is fast emerging as one of the world’s biggest economies. *   About Reuters, opens new tab *   Reuters News Agency, opens new tab *   Reuters and AI, opens new tab *   Reuters Leadership, opens new tab *   Reuters Diversity Report, opens new tab

results.title: DBS, Franklin Templeton, Ripple team up on tokenised money market fund trading - Reuters
results.content: # DBS, Franklin Templeton, Ripple team up on tokenised money market fund trading REUTERS/Dado Ruvic/Illustration Purchase Licensing Rights, opens new tab SINGAPORE, Sept 18 (Reuters) - DBS Group (DBSM.SI), opens new tab has partnered with U.S. asset manager Franklin Templeton (BEN.N), opens new tab and blockchain firm Ripple to offer accredited and institutional investors trading and lending services using tokenised money market funds and Ripple's U.S. dollar stablecoin. Under the agreement, Singapore's biggest lender said that it will list Franklin Templeton's sgBENJI token - the unit of the asset manager's tokenised U.S. dollar money market fund - on the DBS Digital Exchange alongside Ripple's RLUSD stablecoin.

results.title: Singapore's DBS to open 18 new Asian wealth centres, expand advisory push - Reuters
results.content: # Singapore's DBS to open 18 new Asian wealth centres, expand advisory push | Reuters. Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv. ## Browse World. ## Browse Business. ## Browse Markets. # Singapore's DBS to open 18 new Asian wealth centres, expand advisory push. Image 1: A DBS logo on their office building in Singapore. June 1 (Reuters) - DBS Group (DBSM.SI), opens new tab said on Monday it would ‌open 18 new wealth centres across Asia by the end of 2027 and upgrade 36 existing centres over the next 18 months, marking the largest ​physical expansion of its wealth franchise to date. In Singapore, DBS said its Treasures wealth centre ​footprint will increase by 50% with the new openings. *   About Reuters, opens new tab. *   Media Center, opens new tab. *   Reuters News Agency, opens new tab. *   Reuters Leadership, opens new tab. *   #### World-Check, opens new tab.

results.title: Singapore bank DBS keeps 2025 outlook, UOB trims expectations after mixed Q2 results - Reuters
results.content: SINGAPORE, Aug 7 (Reuters) - Singaporean banks posted mixed second-quarter earnings on Thursday, with DBS Group (DBSM.SI), opens new tab maintaining its 2025 outlook and United Overseas Bank (UOBH.SI), opens new tab resuming guidance but trimming expectations amid macroeconomic uncertainty. The banks' results followed that of peer Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp (OCBC.SI), opens new tab which posted on Friday an in-line second-quarter net profit, but cut its 2025 net interest income expectations and flagged persisting tariff uncertainty. Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles., opens new tab *   About Reuters, opens new tab *   Reuters News Agency, opens new tab *   Reuters and AI, opens new tab *   Reuters Leadership, opens new tab *   Reuters Diversity Report, opens new tab

results.title: DBS Debuts AI Coach to Upskill Workforce - Vietnam Investment Review - VIR
results.content: HONG KONG SAR - Media OutReach Newswire - 24 July 2025 - DBS announced today that it is democratising coaching for its workforce with the launch of iCoach, a Generative AI-powered (Gen AI) platform developed in collaboration with Marshall Goldsmith, one of the world's top executive coaches. While most corporations reserve coaching[1] for their top executives or high potential talent, DBS believes that employees at all levels would benefit from coaching by receiving expert advice on career development as well as topical issues such as work-life balance, personal and small team leadership, or mental wellness. Image 4: DBS named world's best bank for sustainable finance by Global Finance Image 5: DBS Bank empowers Vietnam's green revolution

results.title: Singapore bank DBS fourth-quarter net profit falls 10%, misses forecast - Reuters
results.content: # Singapore bank DBS fourth-quarter net profit falls 10%, misses forecast. Reporting by Rae Wee and Yantoultra Ngui; Editing by Cynthia Osterman and Mark Porter. SINGAPORE, Feb 9 (Reuters) - DBS Group (DBSM.SI), opens new tab, Singapore's biggest bank, posted fourth-quarter net profit on Monday that was down 10% over the same period a year earlier, mainly due to lower net interest margin. DBS, which is also Southeast Asia's largest bank by assets, said October-December net profit dropped to S$2.26 billion ($1.78 billion) from S$2.52 billion a year earlier. Yantoultra Ngui is the Southeast Asia Deals Correspondent of Reuters in Singapore, covering M&A and capital market activities in a region that is fast emerging as one of the world’s biggest economies. * ### Singapore bank DBS fourth-quarter net profit falls 10%, misses forecast. DBS Group , Singapore's biggest bank, posted fourth-quarter net profit on Monday that was down 10% over the same period a year earlier, mainly due to lower net interest margin.

results.title: DBS Launches Gen AI-Powered Coaching Tool to Future Proof Its Workforce #DBS - Media OutReach Newswire
results.content: DBS Launches Gen AI-Powered Coaching Tool to Future Proof Its Workforce | Media OutReach Newswire APAC HONG KONG SAR - Media OutReach Newswire - 24 July 2025 - DBS announced today that it is democratising coaching for its workforce with the launch of iCoach, a Generative AI-powered (Gen AI) platform developed in collaboration with Marshall Goldsmith, one of the world's top executive coaches. While most corporations reserve coaching[1] for their top executives or high potential talent, DBS believes that employees at all levels would benefit from coaching by receiving expert advice on career development as well as topical issues such as work-life balance, personal and small team leadership, or mental wellness. With the advent of Gen AI, DBS saw an opportunity to leverage the technology to overcome these challenges, enabling the bank to avail coaching to employees across its network.
